Deep dive into ~/antonio/mms/system/docs/MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md. Since this file is 294KB (~9000 lines), extract:

1. **Table of Contents** - Full structure and chapters
2. **Key sections** - Domain knowledge, market research, UX research, technical decisions
3. **Research findings** - Competitor analysis, railway industry research, best practices
4. **Mathematical/statistical content** - Any formulas, algorithms, calculations
5. **Technology decisions** - ADRs, rationale for choices
6. **Visual design** - Design tokens, color palettes, typography
7. **Demo strategy** - Presentation scripts, wow moments

This is the most comprehensive spec - understand what knowledge it contains for consolidation.

   337‚Üí

   339‚Üí
   340‚Üí# PART I: BUSINESS CONTEXT & STRATEGY
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí---
   343‚Üí
   344‚Üí## Chapter 1: Executive Summary
   345‚Üí
   346‚Üí### 1.1 Project Overview
   347‚Üí
   348‚Üí**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution designed to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets exceeding 100 units. The system bridges the gap between Operations and Maintenance departments through predictive, data-driven planning.
   349‚Üí
   350‚Üí**Core Problem Statement:**
   351‚Üí
   352‚ÜíRailway operators managing large fleets face three critical pain points:
   353‚Üí
   354‚Üí1. **Over-running trains** - Units exceeding target mileage degrade faster, increasing maintenance costs and reducing asset lifespan
   355‚Üí2. **Under-running trains** - Units falling below target mileage represent idle capital with poor return on investment
   356‚Üí3. **Manual planning limitations** - Excel-based planning does not scale for 100+ train fleets and cannot optimize across multiple constraints
   357‚Üí
   358‚Üí**Solution Vision:**
   359‚Üí
   360‚ÜíMMS addresses all three pain points through:
   361‚Üí- Intelligent mileage balancing across the entire fleet
   362‚Üí- AI-powered train recommendations with visible justifications
   363‚Üí- Predictive maintenance scheduling coordinated with operations
   364‚Üí- What-if simulation for strategic planning
   365‚Üí
   366‚Üí### 1.2 Business Objective
   367‚Üí
   368‚Üí**Primary Goal:** Win a competitive bid for a railway fleet management contract through a compelling 15-minute demo presentation.
   369‚Üí
   370‚Üí**Target Audience:**
   371‚Üí- Operations Director (decision maker)
   372‚Üí- Client's CTO (technical validator)
   373‚Üí- Maintenance leadership (domain experts)
   374‚Üí
   375‚Üí**Success Statement:**
   376‚Üí
   377‚Üí> After 15 minutes of demo, the client should think: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**
   378‚Üí
   379‚Üí**Key Differentiators from Competitors:**
   380‚Üí
   381‚Üí| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
   382‚Üí|----------------|----------------|
   383‚Üí| Km balancing as PRIMARY objective | Competitors treat this as a secondary feature |
   384‚Üí| Unified Maintenance ‚ÜĒ Operations interface | Eliminates departmental silos, reduces rework |
   385‚Üí| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit and optimizable |
   386‚Üí| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
   387‚Üí| Transparent decision justification | Trust building through explainability, not black box AI |
   388‚Üí
   389‚Üí### 1.3 Success Metrics
   390‚Üí
   391‚Üí**User Success Metrics:**
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
   394‚Üí|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   395‚Üí| Planner | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
   396‚Üí| Planner | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
   397‚Üí| Planner | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
   398‚Üí| Dispatcher | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
   399‚Üí| Dispatcher | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
   400‚Üí| Manager | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
   401‚Üí| All Users | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |
   402‚Üí
   403‚Üí**Business Success Metrics:**
   404‚Üí
   405‚Üí| Metric | Target |
   406‚Üí|--------|--------|
   407‚Üí| Demo Duration | 15 minutes maximum |
   408‚Üí| Demo Stability | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures |
   409‚Üí| Domain Terminology | 100% correct EN 15380 railway terms |
   410‚Üí| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" |
   411‚Üí
   412‚Üí**Technical Success Metrics:**
   413‚Üí
   414‚Üí| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
   415‚Üí|-------------|--------|-----------|
   416‚Üí| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
   417‚Üí| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in live demo |
   418‚Üí| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
   419‚Üí| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactive feel |
   420‚Üí| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Critical for presentation |
   421‚Üí
   422‚Üí### 1.4 Timeline & Constraints
   423‚Üí
   424‚Üí**Project Timeline:** 12 weeks
   425‚Üí
   426‚Üí**Milestone Schedule:**
   427‚Üí
   428‚Üí| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
   429‚Üí|------|-----------|-------------|
   430‚Üí| Week 4 | Core solver working | Proof of technical capability |
   431‚Üí| Week 6 | Navigable UI with mocked data | Visual credibility established |
   432‚Üí| Week 8 | End-to-end flow working | Integration proof complete |
   433‚Üí| Week 10 | Specialist validation passed | Domain credibility confirmed |
   434‚Üí| Week 12 | Demo-ready with fallbacks | Risk mitigation complete |
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí**Technical Constraints:**
   437‚Üí
   438‚Üí| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
   439‚Üí|------------|--------|------------|
   440‚Üí| 12-week timeline | Limited feature scope | Focus exclusively on P0 features |
   441‚Üí| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Use seed data, skip authentication |
   442‚Üí| Python solver requirement | Cross-language integration | JSON REST API between services |
   443‚Üí| OR-Tools learning curve | Development complexity | Simplify model, use greedy fallback |
   444‚Üí
   445‚Üí**Key Assumptions:**
   446‚Üí
   447‚Üí1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
   448‚Üí2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
   449‚Üí3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support required
   450‚Üí4. English UI only (no internationalization)
   451‚Üí5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)
   452‚Üí
   453‚Üí---
   454‚Üí
   455‚Üí## Chapter 2: Railway Domain Fundamentals
   456‚Üí
   457‚Üí### 2.1 Essential Glossary
   458‚Üí
   459‚ÜíAll UI labels and system messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology. Using incorrect terms destroys credibility with railway professionals.
   460‚Üí
   461‚Üí**Core Terms:**
   462‚Üí
   463‚Üí| Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   464‚Üí|------|------------|----------------|
   465‚Üí| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance (trains, carriages, locomotives) | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   466‚Üí| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing the same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile | Group, Class, Collection |
   467‚Üí| Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   468‚Üí| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness status (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   469‚Üí| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/services | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   470‚Üí| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   471‚Üí| Service | A scheduled revenue-generating journey from origin to destination | Route, Trip, Run |

---

# PART I: BUSINESS CONTEXT & STRATEGY

---

## Chapter 1: Executive Summary

### 1.1 Project Overview

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution designed to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets exceeding 100 units. The system bridges the gap between Operations and Maintenance departments through predictive, data-driven planning.

**Core Problem Statement:**

Railway operators managing large fleets face three critical pain points:

1. **Over-running trains** - Units exceeding target mileage degrade faster, increasing maintenance costs and reducing asset lifespan
2. **Under-running trains** - Units falling below target mileage represent idle capital with poor return on investment
3. **Manual planning limitations** - Excel-based planning does not scale for 100+ train fleets and cannot optimize across multiple constraints

**Solution Vision:**

MMS addresses all three pain points through:
- Intelligent mileage balancing across the entire fleet
- AI-powered train recommendations with visible justifications
- Predictive maintenance scheduling coordinated with operations
- What-if simulation for strategic planning

### 1.2 Business Objective

**Primary Goal:** Win a competitive bid for a railway fleet management contract through a compelling 15-minute demo presentation.

**Target Audience:**
- Operations Director (decision maker)
- Client's CTO (technical validator)
- Maintenance leadership (domain experts)

**Success Statement:**

> After 15 minutes of demo, the client should think: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**

**Key Differentiators from Competitors:**

| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
|----------------|----------------|
| Km balancing as PRIMARY objective | Competitors treat this as a secondary feature |
| Unified Maintenance ‚ÜĒ Operations interface | Eliminates departmental silos, reduces rework |
| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit and optimizable |
| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
| Transparent decision justification | Trust building through explainability, not black box AI |

### 1.3 Success Metrics

**User Success Metrics:**

| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| Planner | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
| Planner | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
| Planner | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
| Dispatcher | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
| Dispatcher | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
| Manager | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
| All Users | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |

**Business Success Metrics:**

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Demo Duration | 15 minutes maximum |
| Demo Stability | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures |
| Domain Terminology | 100% correct EN 15380 railway terms |
| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" |

**Technical Success Metrics:**

| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in live demo |
| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactive feel |
| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Critical for presentation |

### 1.4 Timeline & Constraints

**Project Timeline:** 12 weeks

**Milestone Schedule:**

| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Week 4 | Core solver working | Proof of technical capability |
| Week 6 | Navigable UI with mocked data | Visual credibility established |
| Week 8 | End-to-end flow working | Integration proof complete |
| Week 10 | Specialist validation passed | Domain credibility confirmed |
| Week 12 | Demo-ready with fallbacks | Risk mitigation complete |

**Technical Constraints:**

| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
|------------|--------|------------|
| 12-week timeline | Limited feature scope | Focus exclusively on P0 features |
| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Use seed data, skip authentication |
| Python solver requirement | Cross-language integration | JSON REST API between services |
| OR-Tools learning curve | Development complexity | Simplify model, use greedy fallback |

**Key Assumptions:**

1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support required
4. English UI only (no internationalization)
5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)

---

## Chapter 2: Railway Domain Fundamentals

### 2.1 Essential Glossary

All UI labels and system messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology. Using incorrect terms destroys credibility with railway professionals.

**Core Terms:**

| Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance (trains, carriages, locomotives) | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing the same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness status (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/services | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Service | A scheduled revenue-generating journey from origin to destination | Route, Trip, Run |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet by controlling km distribution | Usage balancing, Wear distribution |

**Maintenance Terms:**

| Term | Definition | Typical Interval |
|------|------------|------------------|
| PM1 | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (light inspection) | Every 15,000 km |
| PM2 | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (medium service) | Every 45,000 km |
| HM | Heavy Maintenance (major overhaul) | Every 90,000 km |
| OVH | Overhaul (complete refurbishment) | Every 6 years or 500,000 km |

**Operational Terms:**

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle |
| Overrun | Train that has exceeded its target mileage |
| Underrun | Train that falls short of its target mileage |
| Variance | Statistical measure of mileage distribution spread across fleet |
| Aptitude | Train's certification for specific line/speed requirements |

### 2.2 Business Entities & Relationships

**Entity Relationship Model:**

   595‚Üí### 2.4 Critical Domain Rules (Unknown Unknowns)
   596‚Üí
   597‚ÜíThese are domain constraints that seasoned railway professionals know intuitively but are rarely documented. Violating these rules destroys credibility instantly.
   598‚Üí
   599‚Üí**Hard Constraints (Never Violate):**
   600‚Üí
   601‚Üí1. **Unfit trains are NEVER assigned to service** - No exception, no override
   602‚Üí2. **Maintenance windows are sacred** - A train scheduled for PM cannot be reassigned
   603‚Üí3. **Aptitude requirements are binary** - A train either can or cannot run on a line
   604‚Üí4. **Depot capacity is physical** - You cannot exceed track slots, period
   605‚Üí5. **Service cannot be cancelled to balance km** - Operations > Optimization
   606‚Üí
   607‚Üí**Soft Constraints (Optimize Within):**
   608‚Üí
   609‚Üí1. **Km balancing priority** - Under-running trains should get high-km services
   610‚Üí2. **Dead running minimization** - Prefer trains that end near next service start
   611‚Üí3. **Maintenance depot proximity** - Schedule services that end near maintenance depot
   612‚Üí4. **Crew familiarity** - Same crews prefer same train types (not modeled in MVP)
   613‚Üí
   614‚Üí**Critical Timing Rules:**
   615‚Üí
   616‚Üí| Rule | Specification |
   617‚Üí|------|---------------|
   618‚Üí| PM1 tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī500 km from trigger |
   619‚Üí| PM2 tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī1,000 km from trigger |
   620‚Üí| HM tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī2,000 km from trigger |
   621‚Üí| Overrun alert | Train at 95% of next PM triggers warning |
   622‚Üí| Critical overrun | Train at 105% of next PM triggers alert |
   623‚Üí
   624‚Üí### 2.5 ECM Certification Framework (EU Regulation 2019/779)
   625‚Üí
   626‚ÜíMMS aligns with the Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM) certification requirements as defined by EU Regulation 2019/779. This framework establishes the legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance across the European railway network.
   627‚Üí
   628‚Üí**ECM Function Mapping:**
   629‚Üí
   630‚Üí| Function | Responsibility | MMS Feature |
   631‚Üí|----------|----------------|-------------|
   632‚Üí| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking, audit trail |
   633‚Üí| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Scheduler optimization, plan generation, interval definitions |
   634‚Üí| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection, availability monitoring |
   635‚Üí| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order generation, depot allocation, resource planning |
   636‚Üí
   637‚Üí**Compliance Mapping:**
   638‚Üí
   639‚Üí| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS Implementation |
   640‚Üí|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
   641‚Üí| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings, overrun alerts |
   642‚Üí| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Future: resource skill tracking (Phase 2+) |
   643‚Üí| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Audit trail, maintenance history, decision logging |
   644‚Üí| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI dashboard, fleet availability metrics, variance tracking |
   645‚Üí
   646‚Üí**Critical Note:** The ECM framework requires that the entity responsible for maintenance (operator or subcontractor) maintains full traceability of all maintenance decisions. MMS provides this through:
   647‚Üí- Immutable audit logs of all scheduling decisions
   648‚Üí- Justification records for allocation recommendations
   649‚Üí- Historical mileage tracking with timestamps
   650‚Üí
   651‚Üí### 2.6 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   652‚Üí
   653‚ÜíMMS supports RAMS (Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety) principles as defined in EN 50126-1, the European standard for railway dependability.
   654‚Üí
   655‚Üí**RAMS Integration:**
   656‚Üí
   657‚Üí| Aspect | Standard | MMS Feature | Target |
   658‚Üí|--------|----------|-------------|--------|
   659‚Üí| **Reliability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß6 | MTBF tracking, failure prediction | TBD by operator |
   660‚Üí| **Availability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß7 | Fleet availability KPIs, downtime monitoring | 95-99% |
   661‚Üí| **Maintainability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß8 | MTTR optimization, workload balancing | <24h per PM1 |
   662‚Üí| **Safety** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß9 | Critical task prioritization, overrun prevention | Zero safety overruns |
   663‚Üí
   664‚Üí**Key Performance Indicators:**
   665‚Üí
   666‚Üí| KPI | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   667‚Üí|-----|---------|--------|-------------------|
   668‚Üí| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) √ó 100 | ‚Č•97% | <95% triggers alert |
   669‚Üí| MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | TBD | Operator-defined |
   670‚Üí| MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) | Total Downtime / Number of Repairs | ‚Č§24h | >48h triggers escalation |
   671‚Üí| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time Maintenance / Scheduled Maintenance) √ó 100 | ‚Č•95% | <90% requires review |
   672‚Üí
   673‚Üí### 2.7 Terminology Equivalence
   674‚Üí
   675‚ÜíMMS uses aviation-derived terminology (A/B/C/D Checks) internally for developer familiarity, but provides full equivalence mapping to railway standards for operator communication.
   676‚Üí
   677‚Üí**Maintenance Level Equivalence:**
   678‚Üí
   679‚Üí| MMS Term | Railway Standard (EN 15380) | Aviation Origin | Typical Interval | Duration |
   680‚Üí|----------|----------------------------|-----------------|--------------------|----------|
   681‚Üí| A-Check | PM1 (Daily/Service) | Line maintenance | Daily / 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
   682‚Üí| B-Check | PM2 (Intermediate) | A-Check | 45,000-50,000 km | 4-16 hours |
   683‚Üí| C-Check | PM3 (Scheduled) | C-Check | 90,000-100,000 km | 1-3 days |
   684‚Üí| D-Overhaul | HM (Heavy Maintenance) | D-Check | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |
   685‚Üí
   686‚Üí> **Note:** MMS uses aviation-derived terminology (A/B/C/D) internally for developer familiarity. All user-facing displays and reports use EN 15380 railway terminology (PM1/PM2/PM3/HM) to maintain credibility with railway professionals.
   687‚Üí
   688‚Üí**UIC IRS Reference:**
   689‚Üí
   690‚ÜíThe International Union of Railways (UIC) publishes International Railway Solutions (IRS) that complement EN standards:
   691‚Üí
   692‚Üí| Document | Title | Relevance to MMS |
   693‚Üí|----------|-------|------------------|
   694‚Üí| IRS 30100-3 | Maintenance Terms | Terminology standardization |
   695‚Üí| IRS 30200 | RAM Demonstrable values | KPI calculation methods |
   696‚Üí| IRS 70711 | Rolling Stock Maintenance | Maintenance interval guidelines |
   697‚Üí

   720‚Üí- This is NOT a revenue management system

   739‚Üí### 3.2 SignatureRail Deep Analysis

   744‚Üí
   745‚Üí- Clean, modern enterprise UI
   746‚Üí- Well-organized workflow panels
   747‚Üí- Good use of data tables
   748‚Üí- Professional color scheme
   749‚Üí- Clear navigation structure
   750‚Üí
   751‚Üí**SignatureRail Weaknesses (MMS Opportunities):**
   752‚Üí
   753‚Üí1. **Basic km tracking without optimization** - Shows variance but doesn't recommend actions
   754‚Üí2. **No visible justifications** - Recommendations are black box
   755‚Üí3. **Limited simulation capability** - Cannot run what-if scenarios
   756‚Üí4. **Separate maintenance and operations** - Silos remain
   757‚Üí5. **No map visualization** - Spatial context missing

   802‚Üí## Chapter 4: Demo Strategy & Presentation
   803‚Üí
   804‚Üí### 4.1 Core Principle: Depth Over Breadth
   805‚Üí
   806‚Üí> **Mantra:** "Solving 1 pain point extremely well > solving 10 pain points poorly"
   807‚Üí
   808‚Üí**Strategic Focus:**
   809‚Üí
   810‚ÜíThe demo should demonstrate deep understanding of the km balancing problem rather than showcase many features superficially. Every feature shown must:
   811‚Üí
   812‚Üí1. Be 100% functional (no "coming soon")
   813‚Üí2. Use correct railway terminology
   814‚Üí3. Show real (simulated but realistic) data
   815‚Üí4. Complete end-to-end without errors
   816‚Üí
   817‚Üí### 4.2 Wow Moments
   818‚Üí
   819‚ÜíThese are specific moments in the demo designed to create impact:
   820‚Üí
   821‚Üí**Wow Moment 1: The Variance Discovery**
   822‚Üí- Dashboard loads showing 18% variance (yellow warning)
   823‚Üí- Client recognizes their exact problem
   824‚Üí- "They're showing us what we live every day"
   825‚Üí
   826‚Üí**Wow Moment 2: The Justification Card**
   827‚Üí- Recommendation shows not just WHAT but WHY
   828‚Üí- 3+ human-readable reasons
   829‚Üí- "Finally, a system that explains itself"
   830‚Üí
   831‚Üí**Wow Moment 3: The What-If**
   832‚Üí- Create scenario, adjust allocations
   833‚Üí- See projected impact in < 10 seconds
   834‚Üí- "I can test my ideas before committing"
   835‚Üí
   836‚Üí**Wow Moment 4: The Map Context**
   837‚Üí- See where all trains are spatially
   838‚Üí- Click train, see km delta immediately
   839‚Üí- "This gives us operational awareness"
   840‚Üí
   841‚Üí### 4.3 Credibility Checklist
   842‚Üí
   843‚ÜíBefore demo, verify:
   844‚Üí
   845‚Üí- [ ] All terminology is EN 15380 compliant
   846‚Üí- [ ] Depot names are real Portuguese locations
   847‚Üí- [ ] Service codes follow realistic patterns (IC, R, U)
   848‚Üí- [ ] Maintenance intervals are realistic (PM1: 15k km)
   849‚Üí- [ ] Train IDs follow realistic naming (UTE-3400-xxx)
   850‚Üí- [ ] Km values are in plausible ranges (30k-60k)
   851‚Üí- [ ] Variance percentages are realistic (10-25%)
   852‚Üí- [ ] Map shows Portugal correctly positioned
   853‚Üí
   854‚Üí### 4.4 Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   855‚Üí
   856‚Üí**NEVER do these in demo:**
   857‚Üí
   858‚Üí| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Alternative |
   859‚Üí|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   860‚Üí| Show stub screens | Destroys credibility | Show fewer, complete features |
   861‚Üí| Use placeholder data | "Lorem ipsum" is unprofessional | Use realistic railway data |
   862‚Üí| Demo features that don't work | Client will try to break it | Only show stable features |
   863‚Üí| Use generic software terms | "Vehicle" instead of "Train" | Use railway terminology |
   864‚Üí| Show loading spinners | Suggests slow system | Pre-cache or mock fast responses |
   865‚Üí| Apologize for missing features | Shows lack of confidence | Present what exists as intentional |
   866‚Üí
   867‚Üí### 4.5 Presentation Script (15 minutes)
   868‚Üí
   869‚Üí**Minutes 0-2: Context Setting**
   870‚Üí- "We understand your km balancing challenge"
   871‚Üí- Show dashboard with variance indicator
   872‚Üí- "This is what you see every day"

   877‚Üí- "These 9 trains are costing you money"

   880‚Üí- Navigate to Allocation

   882‚Üí- Show recommendations with justifications
   883‚Üí- "Not just WHAT to assign, but WHY"
   884‚Üí- Confirm allocation with one click

   886‚Üí**Minutes 8-11: Planning Ahead**
   887‚Üí- Navigate to Simulation
   888‚Üí- Create what-if scenario
   889‚Üí- Adjust allocations for under-running trains
   890‚Üí- Show projected variance improvement
   891‚Üí- "Test your strategy before committing"
   892‚Üí
   893‚Üí**Minutes 11-13: The Big Picture**
   894‚Üí- Return to Dashboard
   895‚Üí- Show map with train positions
   896‚Üí- Show depot capacity
   897‚Üí- "Everything in one view"
   898‚Üí
   899‚Üí**Minutes 13-15: Q&A Buffer**
   900‚Üí- Prepared questions if no questions asked
   901‚Üí- Have fallback screens ready

**Cardinality Rules:**

- One Fleet has many Trains (1:N)
- One Train belongs to one Fleet (N:1)
- One Depot can host trains from multiple Fleets (N:M)
- One Train has one Base Depot (N:1)
- One Service can have many Allocations (different dates) (1:N)
- One Train can have many Allocations (different services) (1:N)
- One Train can have many Maintenance Schedules (1:N)

### 2.4 Critical Domain Rules (Unknown Unknowns)

These are domain constraints that seasoned railway professionals know intuitively but are rarely documented. Violating these rules destroys credibility instantly.

**Hard Constraints (Never Violate):**

1. **Unfit trains are NEVER assigned to service** - No exception, no override
2. **Maintenance windows are sacred** - A train scheduled for PM cannot be reassigned
3. **Aptitude requirements are binary** - A train either can or cannot run on a line
4. **Depot capacity is physical** - You cannot exceed track slots, period
5. **Service cannot be cancelled to balance km** - Operations > Optimization

**Soft Constraints (Optimize Within):**

1. **Km balancing priority** - Under-running trains should get high-km services
2. **Dead running minimization** - Prefer trains that end near next service start
3. **Maintenance depot proximity** - Schedule services that end near maintenance depot
4. **Crew familiarity** - Same crews prefer same train types (not modeled in MVP)

**Critical Timing Rules:**

| Rule | Specification |
|------|---------------|
| PM1 tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī500 km from trigger |
| PM2 tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī1,000 km from trigger |
| HM tolerance window | Can be ¬Ī2,000 km from trigger |
| Overrun alert | Train at 95% of next PM triggers warning |
| Critical overrun | Train at 105% of next PM triggers alert |

### 2.5 ECM Certification Framework (EU Regulation 2019/779)

MMS aligns with the Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM) certification requirements as defined by EU Regulation 2019/779. This framework establishes the legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance across the European railway network.

**ECM Function Mapping:**

| Function | Responsibility | MMS Feature |
|----------|----------------|-------------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking, audit trail |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Scheduler optimization, plan generation, interval definitions |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection, availability monitoring |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order generation, depot allocation, resource planning |

**Compliance Mapping:**

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings, overrun alerts |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Future: resource skill tracking (Phase 2+) |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Audit trail, maintenance history, decision logging |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI dashboard, fleet availability metrics, variance tracking |

**Critical Note:** The ECM framework requires that the entity responsible for maintenance (operator or subcontractor) maintains full traceability of all maintenance decisions. MMS provides this through:
- Immutable audit logs of all scheduling decisions
- Justification records for allocation recommendations
- Historical mileage tracking with timestamps

### 2.6 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

MMS supports RAMS (Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety) principles as defined in EN 50126-1, the European standard for railway dependability.

**RAMS Integration:**

| Aspect | Standard | MMS Feature | Target |
|--------|----------|-------------|--------|
| **Reliability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß6 | MTBF tracking, failure prediction | TBD by operator |
| **Availability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß7 | Fleet availability KPIs, downtime monitoring | 95-99% |
| **Maintainability** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß8 | MTTR optimization, workload balancing | <24h per PM1 |
| **Safety** | EN 50126-1 ¬ß9 | Critical task prioritization, overrun prevention | Zero safety overruns |

**Key Performance Indicators:**

| KPI | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|-----|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) √ó 100 | ‚Č•97% | <95% triggers alert |
| MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | TBD | Operator-defined |
| MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) | Total Downtime / Number of Repairs | ‚Č§24h | >48h triggers escalation |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time Maintenance / Scheduled Maintenance) √ó 100 | ‚Č•95% | <90% requires review |

### 2.7 Terminology Equivalence

MMS uses aviation-derived terminology (A/B/C/D Checks) internally for developer familiarity, but provides full equivalence mapping to railway standards for operator communication.

**Maintenance Level Equivalence:**

| MMS Term | Railway Standard (EN 15380) | Aviation Origin | Typical Interval | Duration |
|----------|----------------------------|-----------------|--------------------|----------|
| A-Check | PM1 (Daily/Service) | Line maintenance | Daily / 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
| B-Check | PM2 (Intermediate) | A-Check | 45,000-50,000 km | 4-16 hours |
| C-Check | PM3 (Scheduled) | C-Check | 90,000-100,000 km | 1-3 days |
| D-Overhaul | HM (Heavy Maintenance) | D-Check | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |

> **Note:** MMS uses aviation-derived terminology (A/B/C/D) internally for developer familiarity. All user-facing displays and reports use EN 15380 railway terminology (PM1/PM2/PM3/HM) to maintain credibility with railway professionals.

**UIC IRS Reference:**

The International Union of Railways (UIC) publishes International Railway Solutions (IRS) that complement EN standards:

| Document | Title | Relevance to MMS |
|----------|-------|------------------|
| IRS 30100-3 | Maintenance Terms | Terminology standardization |
| IRS 30200 | RAM Demonstrable values | KPI calculation methods |
| IRS 70711 | Rolling Stock Maintenance | Maintenance interval guidelines |

### 2.8 What the System Does NOT Do

Explicit scope boundaries to prevent scope creep and set correct expectations:

**Phase 2+ (Not in Demo):**

| Feature | Reason for Exclusion | Target Phase |
|---------|---------------------|--------------|
| MILP long-term optimization | Exceeds complexity budget | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM system integration | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption management | Different architecture needed | Phase 3 |
| Integrated crew scheduling | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native mobile application | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-language support | English sufficient for demo | Production |
| Real-time GPS tracking | Simulated positions sufficient | Production |
| Predictive maintenance ML | Beyond demo scope | Phase 3 |

**Explicitly NOT Goals:**

- This is NOT a full EAM (Enterprise Asset Management) system
- This is NOT a crew scheduling system
- This is NOT a timetabling system
- This is NOT a revenue management system
- This does NOT handle disruption/delay management
- This does NOT integrate with signaling systems

---

## Chapter 3: Market Analysis & Competitive Positioning

### 3.1 Competitor Landscape

**Primary Competitors:**

| Competitor | Focus Area | Strength | Weakness |
|------------|------------|----------|----------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver, enterprise scale | Complex, expensive, long implementation |
| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling, UK market presence | Less focus on maintenance integration |
| Railnova | Rail fleet telematics | Real-time data, IoT integration | Less sophisticated optimization |
| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, workflow focus | Limited optimization, basic balancing |

### 3.2 SignatureRail Deep Analysis

SignatureRail represents the most direct competitor in terms of user experience and feature set.

**SignatureRail Strengths:**

- Clean, modern enterprise UI
- Well-organized workflow panels
- Good use of data tables
- Professional color scheme
- Clear navigation structure

**SignatureRail Weaknesses (MMS Opportunities):**

1. **Basic km tracking without optimization** - Shows variance but doesn't recommend actions
2. **No visible justifications** - Recommendations are black box
3. **Limited simulation capability** - Cannot run what-if scenarios
4. **Separate maintenance and operations** - Silos remain
5. **No map visualization** - Spatial context missing

### 3.3 Market Gaps & Opportunities

**Gap Analysis:**

| Gap | Market Need | MMS Solution |
|-----|-------------|--------------|
| Passive vs Active Balancing | Systems show variance but don't optimize | AI-powered recommendations with justifications |
| Black Box Decisions | Users don't understand why system recommends | Transparent 3+ reason justifications |
| Operations/Maintenance Silos | Separate systems, manual coordination | Unified interface for both departments |
| Strategic Planning | Day-to-day focus, no simulation | What-if scenarios for planning |
| Dead Running Visibility | Hidden cost, rarely tracked | Explicit display and optimization |

### 3.4 MMS Differentiators

**Primary Differentiators:**

1. **Km Balancing as Core Objective**
   - Not a secondary feature or afterthought
   - Drives all allocation recommendations
   - Visible in dashboard KPIs

2. **Transparent Justifications**
   - Every recommendation shows 3+ reasons
   - Human-readable explanations
   - Builds trust vs black box

3. **Unified Maintenance ‚ÜĒ Operations**
   - Single interface for both departments
   - Recommendations consider maintenance schedule
   - Reduces coordination overhead

4. **Accessible Simulation**
   - Decision-makers can test scenarios
   - Side-by-side comparison
   - No technical expertise required

5. **Visible Dead Running**
   - Hidden cost made explicit
   - Factored into optimization
   - ROI clearly demonstrable

---

## Chapter 4: Demo Strategy & Presentation

### 4.1 Core Principle: Depth Over Breadth

> **Mantra:** "Solving 1 pain point extremely well > solving 10 pain points poorly"

**Strategic Focus:**

The demo should demonstrate deep understanding of the km balancing problem rather than showcase many features superficially. Every feature shown must:

1. Be 100% functional (no "coming soon")
2. Use correct railway terminology
3. Show real (simulated but realistic) data
4. Complete end-to-end without errors

### 4.2 Wow Moments

These are specific moments in the demo designed to create impact:

**Wow Moment 1: The Variance Discovery**
- Dashboard loads showing 18% variance (yellow warning)
- Client recognizes their exact problem
- "They're showing us what we live every day"

**Wow Moment 2: The Justification Card**
- Recommendation shows not just WHAT but WHY
- 3+ human-readable reasons
- "Finally, a system that explains itself"

**Wow Moment 3: The What-If**
- Create scenario, adjust allocations
- See projected impact in < 10 seconds
- "I can test my ideas before committing"

**Wow Moment 4: The Map Context**
- See where all trains are spatially
- Click train, see km delta immediately
- "This gives us operational awareness"

### 4.3 Credibility Checklist

Before demo, verify:

- [ ] All terminology is EN 15380 compliant
- [ ] Depot names are real Portuguese locations
- [ ] Service codes follow realistic patterns (IC, R, U)
- [ ] Maintenance intervals are realistic (PM1: 15k km)
- [ ] Train IDs follow realistic naming (UTE-3400-xxx)
- [ ] Km values are in plausible ranges (30k-60k)
- [ ] Variance percentages are realistic (10-25%)
- [ ] Map shows Portugal correctly positioned

### 4.4 Anti-Patterns to Avoid

**NEVER do these in demo:**

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Alternative |
|--------------|--------------|-------------|
| Show stub screens | Destroys credibility | Show fewer, complete features |
| Use placeholder data | "Lorem ipsum" is unprofessional | Use realistic railway data |
| Demo features that don't work | Client will try to break it | Only show stable features |
| Use generic software terms | "Vehicle" instead of "Train" | Use railway terminology |
| Show loading spinners | Suggests slow system | Pre-cache or mock fast responses |
| Apologize for missing features | Shows lack of confidence | Present what exists as intentional |

### 4.5 Presentation Script (15 minutes)

**Minutes 0-2: Context Setting**
- "We understand your km balancing challenge"
- Show dashboard with variance indicator
- "This is what you see every day"

**Minutes 2-5: The Problem Quantified**
- Click variance to see distribution
- Show outliers (5 over-running, 4 under-running)
- "These 9 trains are costing you money"

**Minutes 5-8: The Solution in Action**
- Navigate to Allocation
- Select a pending service
- Show recommendations with justifications
- "Not just WHAT to assign, but WHY"
- Confirm allocation with one click

**Minutes 8-11: Planning Ahead**
- Navigate to Simulation
- Create what-if scenario
- Adjust allocations for under-running trains
- Show projected variance improvement
- "Test your strategy before committing"

**Minutes 11-13: The Big Picture**
- Return to Dashboard
- Show map with train positions
- Show depot capacity
- "Everything in one view"

**Minutes 13-15: Q&A Buffer**
- Prepared questions if no questions asked
- Have fallback screens ready

### 4.6 FAQ & Fallback Plans

**Anticipated Questions:**

| Question | Prepared Answer |
|----------|-----------------|
| "How does the solver work?" | "CP-SAT optimization balancing multiple constraints - we can show the logic" |
| "Can it integrate with our EAM?" | "Yes, REST API design allows integration with any EAM system" |
| "What about real-time GPS?" | "Architecture supports real-time feeds, demo uses simulated positions" |
| "How long to implement?" | "Core system in 3 months, customization depends on requirements" |
| "What if solver times out?" | "Fallback to simpler ranking ensures always having recommendations" |

**Fallback Plans:**

| Failure | Fallback |
|---------|----------|
| Solver timeout | Pre-computed recommendations in database |
| Database connection lost | Static JSON fallback data |
| Map fails to load | Table view of train positions |
| Browser crash | Second laptop with same setup |
| Network issues | Everything runs locally |

---

## Chapter 5: User Personas

### 5.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner

**Profile:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Name | Paul |
| Age | 45 years |
| Role | Maintenance Planner |
| Experience | 18 years in railway maintenance |
| Education | Mechanical Engineering degree |

**Work Context:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Daily System Usage | 4-8 hours |
| Primary Device | Desktop with large monitor (27"+) |
| Secondary Device | Tablet for depot floor checks |
| Work Hours | 07:00 - 16:00 (day shift) |
| Team Size | Reports to Maintenance Manager, works with 3 other planners |

**Goals:**

1. Schedule preventive maintenance minimizing operational impact
2. Balance depot capacity across weeks
3. Ensure no train exceeds maintenance tolerance windows
4. Coordinate with Operations to minimize service disruption
5. Reduce dead running for maintenance movements

**Frustrations:**

1. "I spend hours in Excel trying to balance the schedule"
2. "Operations changes plans without telling me"
3. "I can't see impact of changes until it's too late"
4. "Every depot is managed separately, no fleet-wide view"
5. "Constant firefighting because maintenance gets pushed"

**Success Moment:**
> "I can see the whole fleet's km distribution and immediately know which trains need attention."

**Critical Need:** Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality showing depot capacity

  2409‚Üí## Part III: Functional Requirements
  2410‚Üí
  2411‚Üí### Chapter 10: Requirements Overview
  2412‚Üí
  2413‚ÜíThis chapter establishes the complete requirements framework for MMS, organizing functional and non-functional requirements with clear priority classifications.
  2414‚Üí
  2415‚Üí#### 10.1 Functional Requirements Matrix
  2416‚Üí
  2417‚Üí**Core Requirements (P0 - Must Have)**
  2418‚Üí
  2419‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
  2420‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|------------|
  2421‚Üí| FR-001 | Display fleet-wide KPI summary on dashboard | Dashboard | As a Fleet Manager, I need to see fleet health at a glance |
  2422‚Üí| FR-002 | Show km variance indicator with threshold alerts | Dashboard | As a Planner, I need to spot imbalances immediately |
  2423‚Üí| FR-003 | Display train positions on interactive map | Map | As a Dispatcher, I need to see where trains are |
  2424‚Üí| FR-004 | Show depot locations with capacity indicators | Map | As a Planner, I need to see depot availability |
  2425‚Üí| FR-005 | Generate train recommendations for service allocation | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need suggestions ranked by suitability |
  2426‚Üí| FR-006 | Display justification reasons for recommendations | Allocation | As a User, I need to understand WHY a train is recommended |
  2427‚Üí| FR-007 | Confirm allocation with single action | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need to allocate quickly |
  2428‚Üí| FR-008 | Visualize km distribution across fleet | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to see the spread |
  2429‚Üí| FR-009 | Identify over-running and under-running trains | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to find outliers |
  2430‚Üí| FR-010 | Create and run what-if scenarios | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to test decisions before committing |
  2431‚Üí| FR-011 | Compare scenarios side-by-side | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to evaluate trade-offs |
  2432‚Üí
  2433‚Üí**Enhancement Requirements (P1 - Should Have)**
  2434‚Üí
  2435‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
  2436‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|------------|
  2437‚Üí| FR-012 | Display maintenance timeline in Gantt view | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to see scheduled maintenances |
  2438‚Üí| FR-013 | Color-code maintenance types (PM1/PM2/HM) | Gantt | As a User, I need visual distinction of maintenance types |
  2439‚Üí| FR-014 | Enable drag-drop rescheduling | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to adjust schedules visually |
  2440‚Üí| FR-015 | Validate scheduling constraints on drag | Gantt | As a User, I need feedback on invalid moves |
  2441‚Üí| FR-016 | Show depot capacity utilization heatmap | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to avoid overbooking depots |
  2442‚Üí| FR-017 | Filter trains by fleet, depot, status | Fleet | As a User, I need to narrow down train lists |
  2443‚Üí| FR-018 | Display train history chart | Details | As a Planner, I need to see km trends over time |
  2444‚Üí| FR-019 | Show upcoming maintenance for each train | Details | As a Planner, I need to see what's due |
  2445‚Üí| FR-020 | Visual alerts for threshold breaches | All | As a User, I need attention drawn to problems |
  2446‚Üí
  2447‚Üí**Nice-to-Have Requirements (P2 - Could Have)**
  2448‚Üí
  2449‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
  2450‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|------------|
  2451‚Üí| FR-021 | Export data to CSV/Excel | All | As a User, I need to share data externally |
  2452‚Üí| FR-022 | Print-friendly dashboard summary | Dashboard | As a Manager, I need paper reports for meetings |
  2453‚Üí| FR-023 | Wheelset km balancing visualization | Balancing | As a Planner, I need component-level detail |
  2454‚Üí| FR-024 | Dead running distance reporting | Reports | As a Planner, I need to minimize empty km |
  2455‚Üí
  2456‚Üí#### 10.2 Non-Functional Requirements
  2457‚Üí
  2458‚Üí**Performance Requirements**
  2459‚Üí
  2460‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
  2461‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
  2462‚Üí| NFR-001 | Page load time | < 3 seconds | Demo credibility, user patience threshold |
  2463‚Üí| NFR-002 | Allocation recommendation | < 30 seconds | Dispatcher workflow efficiency |
  2464‚Üí| NFR-003 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds | Perceived real-time experience |
  2465‚Üí| NFR-004 | Simulation execution | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactivity during demo |
  2466‚Üí| NFR-005 | Gantt render | < 2 seconds | Smooth scrolling expected |
  2467‚Üí| NFR-006 | API response (avg) | < 500ms | UI responsiveness |
  2468‚Üí
  2469‚Üí**Scalability Requirements**
  2470‚Üí
  2471‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
  2472‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
  2473‚Üí| NFR-007 | Train count | 80-120 trains | Typical metro operator fleet |
  2474‚Üí| NFR-008 | Depot count | 12-15 depots | Regional coverage |
  2475‚Üí| NFR-009 | Services per day | 200-300 services | Weekday peak operations |
  2476‚Üí| NFR-010 | Historical data | 12 months | Trend analysis requirements |
  2477‚Üí| NFR-011 | Concurrent users | 10-20 users | Typical operations team size |
  2478‚Üí
  2479‚Üí**Reliability Requirements**
  2480‚Üí
  2481‚Üí| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
  2482‚Üí|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
  2483‚Üí| NFR-012 | Demo stability | 15+ minutes no crashes | Must survive presentation |
  2484‚Üí| NFR-013 | Error recovery | Graceful degradation | Show fallback, not blank screen |

**Drag-Drop Behavior:**

1. User clicks and holds a maintenance block
2. Block becomes draggable, cursor changes
3. Drop zones highlight: green (valid) / red (invalid)
4. On drop, system validates constraints
5. If valid: block moves, toast confirms
6. If invalid: block returns, error explains why

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Gantt displays 4 weeks by default
- [ ] Trains grouped by fleet
- [ ] Maintenance blocks are color-coded by type
- [ ] Hovering block shows details tooltip
- [ ] Drag-drop allows rescheduling
- [ ] Constraints validated in real-time during drag
- [ ] Depot capacity heatmap shows utilization
- [ ] Cells > 90% capacity show warning

## Part III: Functional Requirements

### Chapter 10: Requirements Overview

This chapter establishes the complete requirements framework for MMS, organizing functional and non-functional requirements with clear priority classifications.

#### 10.1 Functional Requirements Matrix

**Core Requirements (P0 - Must Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-001 | Display fleet-wide KPI summary on dashboard | Dashboard | As a Fleet Manager, I need to see fleet health at a glance |
| FR-002 | Show km variance indicator with threshold alerts | Dashboard | As a Planner, I need to spot imbalances immediately |
| FR-003 | Display train positions on interactive map | Map | As a Dispatcher, I need to see where trains are |
| FR-004 | Show depot locations with capacity indicators | Map | As a Planner, I need to see depot availability |
| FR-005 | Generate train recommendations for service allocation | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need suggestions ranked by suitability |
| FR-006 | Display justification reasons for recommendations | Allocation | As a User, I need to understand WHY a train is recommended |
| FR-007 | Confirm allocation with single action | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need to allocate quickly |
| FR-008 | Visualize km distribution across fleet | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to see the spread |
| FR-009 | Identify over-running and under-running trains | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to find outliers |
| FR-010 | Create and run what-if scenarios | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to test decisions before committing |
| FR-011 | Compare scenarios side-by-side | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to evaluate trade-offs |

**Enhancement Requirements (P1 - Should Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-012 | Display maintenance timeline in Gantt view | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to see scheduled maintenances |
| FR-013 | Color-code maintenance types (PM1/PM2/HM) | Gantt | As a User, I need visual distinction of maintenance types |
| FR-014 | Enable drag-drop rescheduling | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to adjust schedules visually |
| FR-015 | Validate scheduling constraints on drag | Gantt | As a User, I need feedback on invalid moves |
| FR-016 | Show depot capacity utilization heatmap | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to avoid overbooking depots |
| FR-017 | Filter trains by fleet, depot, status | Fleet | As a User, I need to narrow down train lists |
| FR-018 | Display train history chart | Details | As a Planner, I need to see km trends over time |
| FR-019 | Show upcoming maintenance for each train | Details | As a Planner, I need to see what's due |
| FR-020 | Visual alerts for threshold breaches | All | As a User, I need attention drawn to problems |

**Nice-to-Have Requirements (P2 - Could Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-021 | Export data to CSV/Excel | All | As a User, I need to share data externally |
| FR-022 | Print-friendly dashboard summary | Dashboard | As a Manager, I need paper reports for meetings |
| FR-023 | Wheelset km balancing visualization | Balancing | As a Planner, I need component-level detail |
| FR-024 | Dead running distance reporting | Reports | As a Planner, I need to minimize empty km |

#### 10.2 Non-Functional Requirements

**Performance Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-001 | Page load time | < 3 seconds | Demo credibility, user patience threshold |
| NFR-002 | Allocation recommendation | < 30 seconds | Dispatcher workflow efficiency |
| NFR-003 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds | Perceived real-time experience |
| NFR-004 | Simulation execution | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactivity during demo |
| NFR-005 | Gantt render | < 2 seconds | Smooth scrolling expected |
| NFR-006 | API response (avg) | < 500ms | UI responsiveness |

**Scalability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-007 | Train count | 80-120 trains | Typical metro operator fleet |
| NFR-008 | Depot count | 12-15 depots | Regional coverage |
| NFR-009 | Services per day | 200-300 services | Weekday peak operations |
| NFR-010 | Historical data | 12 months | Trend analysis requirements |
| NFR-011 | Concurrent users | 10-20 users | Typical operations team size |

**Reliability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-012 | Demo stability | 15+ minutes no crashes | Must survive presentation |
| NFR-013 | Error recovery | Graceful degradation | Show fallback, not blank screen |
| NFR-014 | Data consistency | No corruption | Trust in system integrity |

**Usability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-015 | Click depth | Max 3 clicks to any function | Information accessibility |
| NFR-016 | Learning curve | Basic use within 10 minutes | Demo walkthrough time |
| NFR-017 | Terminology | 100% EN 15380 compliance | Railway domain credibility |
| NFR-018 | Visual feedback | Action confirmation < 300ms | User confidence |

#### 10.3 Priority Classification (P0/P1/P2)

**Priority Definitions**

  2555‚Üí#### 10.4 Scope Boundaries
  2556‚Üí
  2557‚Üí**Explicitly IN Scope**
  2558‚Üí
  2559‚Üí| Area | Included | Notes |
  2560‚Üí|------|----------|-------|
  2561‚Üí| Train Allocation | Single-day allocation workflow | One train to one service |
  2562‚Üí| Km Balancing | Fleet-level visualization | Histogram, rankings, deltas |
  2563‚Üí| Simulation | What-if scenario system | Create, run, compare |
  2564‚Üí| Maintenance View | Read and reschedule | Gantt with drag-drop |
  2565‚Üí| Map | Current positions | Train and depot markers |
  2566‚Üí| Data | Demo dataset | 80-120 trains, seeded |
  2567‚Üí
  2568‚Üí**Explicitly OUT of Scope**
  2569‚Üí
  2570‚Üí| Area | Excluded | Why | Future Phase |
  2571‚Üí|------|----------|-----|--------------|
  2572‚Üí| Multi-Day Optimization | MILP solver for week/month | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
  2573‚Üí| Real EAM Integration | SAP PM, Maximo connections | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
  2574‚Üí| Disruption Management | Real-time failure handling | Different architecture | Phase 3 |
  2575‚Üí| Crew Scheduling | Driver allocation | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
  2576‚Üí| Native Mobile | iOS/Android apps | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
  2577‚Üí| Multi-Language | UI translations | English for demo | Production |
  2578‚Üí| User Authentication | Login, roles, permissions | Demo runs as single user | Production |
  2579‚Üí| Audit Trail | Change history logging | Not visible in demo | Production |
  2580‚Üí| Notification System | Email/SMS alerts | Requires infrastructure | Production |
  2581‚Üí
  2582‚Üí**Scope Control Principle**
  2583‚Üí
  2584‚Üí> "When in doubt, leave it out. It's better to demo 6 features that work perfectly than 12 features with bugs."
  2585‚Üí
  2586‚ÜíThe scope is intentionally constrained to ensure quality within the 12-week timeline. Any scope addition requires removing something of equal effort.

  2680‚Üí#### 11.2 Traffic-Light Indicators
  2681‚Üí
  2682‚ÜíTraffic lights provide instant visual status without requiring interpretation.
  2683‚Üí
  2684‚Üí**Implementation Specification**
  2685‚Üí
  2686‚Üí```typescript
  2687‚Üítype TrafficLightStatus = 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red';
  2688‚Üí
  2689‚Üíinterface TrafficLightConfig {
  2690‚Üí  metric: string;
  2691‚Üí  greenThreshold: number;
  2692‚Üí  yellowThreshold: number;
  2693‚Üí  direction: 'ascending' | 'descending'; // lower is better vs higher is better
  2694‚Üí}
  2695‚Üí
  2696‚Üíconst TRAFFIC_LIGHT_CONFIGS: TrafficLightConfig[] = [
  2697‚Üí  {
  2698‚Üí    metric: 'kmVariance',
  2699‚Üí    greenThreshold: 10,
  2700‚Üí    yellowThreshold: 15,
  2701‚Üí    direction: 'descending', // lower variance is better
  2702‚Üí  },
  2703‚Üí  {
  2704‚Üí    metric: 'fitPercentage',
  2705‚Üí    greenThreshold: 90,
  2706‚Üí    yellowThreshold: 80,
  2707‚Üí    direction: 'ascending', // higher fit % is better
  2708‚Üí  },
  2709‚Üí  {
  2710‚Üí    metric: 'allocationRate',
  2711‚Üí    greenThreshold: 95,
  2712‚Üí    yellowThreshold: 85,
  2713‚Üí    direction: 'ascending', // higher allocation is better
  2714‚Üí  },
  2715‚Üí];
  2716‚Üí```
  2717‚Üí
  2718‚Üí**Visual Representation**
  2719‚Üí
  2720‚Üí```
  2721‚ÜíGreen (ūüüĘ):  Background: #238823, Border: #1a6b1a
  2722‚ÜíYellow (ūüü°): Background: #FFBF00, Border: #cc9900
  2723‚ÜíRed (ūüĒī):    Background: #D2222D, Border: #a61b24

  2725‚Üí
  2726‚Üí**Placement Rules**
  2727‚Üí
  2728‚Üí- Traffic lights appear in the top-right of each KPI card

  2752‚Üí**Capacity Calculation**
  2753‚Üí
  2754‚Üí```typescript
  2755‚Üíinterface DepotCapacity {
  2756‚Üí  depotId: string;
  2757‚Üí  date: Date;
  2758‚Üí  tracksAvailable: number;
  2759‚Üí  tracksScheduled: number;
  2760‚Üí  utilizationPercent: number; // (scheduled / available) * 100
  2761‚Üí}
  2762‚Üí
  2763‚Üífunction getCapacityColor(utilization: number): string {
  2764‚Üí  if (utilization < 50) return COLORS.LOW;      // Light gray
  2765‚Üí  if (utilization < 80) return COLORS.MEDIUM;   // Amber
  2766‚Üí  return COLORS.HIGH;                            // Red
  2767‚Üí}
  2768‚Üí```

  2775‚Üí| Click cell | Navigate to Gantt view filtered to that depot/date |
  2776‚Üí| Click depot name | Navigate to depot detail page |

  2865‚Üí### Chapter 12: Module - Map & Fleet Visualization
  2866‚Üí
  2867‚ÜíThe map provides operational awareness and visual proof of fleet tracking capability.
  2868‚Üí
  2869‚Üí#### 12.1 Map Provider & Configuration
  2870‚Üí
  2871‚Üí**Recommended Provider: Mapbox GL JS**
  2872‚Üí
  2873‚Üí| Aspect | Configuration |
  2874‚Üí|--------|---------------|
  2875‚Üí| Provider | Mapbox GL JS v3 |
  2876‚Üí| Style | Dark mode: `mapbox://styles/mapbox/dark-v11` |
  2877‚Üí| Initial Center | Portugal: `[-8.2245, 40.1356]` |
  2878‚Üí| Initial Zoom | 7 (country-level view) |
  2879‚Üí| Min Zoom | 5 |
  2880‚Üí| Max Zoom | 14 |
  2881‚Üí| Terrain | None (2D only) |
  2882‚Üí
  2883‚Üí**Alternative: Leaflet with Dark Tiles**
  2884‚Üí
  2885‚ÜíIf Mapbox is unavailable, use Leaflet with CartoDB dark tiles:
  2886‚Üí
  2887‚Üí```typescript
  2888‚ÜíL.tileLayer(
  2889‚Üí  'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png',
  2890‚Üí  { attribution: '¬© CartoDB' }
  2891‚Üí);
  2892‚Üí```
  2893‚Üí
  2894‚Üí**Map Container Requirements**
  2895‚Üí
  2896‚Üí- Full width of content area
  2897‚Üí- Minimum height: 500px
  2898‚Üí- Maximum height: 70vh
  2899‚Üí- Border radius: 8px
  2900‚Üí- No scroll inside map (panning only)
  2901‚Üí
  2902‚Üí#### 12.2 Train Markers & Status Colors
  2903‚Üí
  2904‚Üí**Marker Design**
  2905‚Üí
  2906‚Üí```
  2907‚ÜíTrain Marker (16x16px SVG):
  2908‚Üí‚ĒĆ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēź
  2909‚Üí‚Ēā ‚Ė∂‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ  ‚Ēā  (Simplified train icon)
  2910‚Üí‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēė

  2912‚Üí
  2913‚Üí**Status Color Mapping**
  2914‚Üí
  2915‚Üí| Status | Color | Hex Code | Meaning |
  2916‚Üí|--------|-------|----------|---------|
  2917‚Üí| Fit (In Service) | Green | `#2D8A4E` | Operating normally |
  2918‚Üí| Fit (Available) | Blue | `#2196F3` | Ready for allocation |
  2919‚Üí| Unfit | Red | `#E63946` | Requires maintenance |

  2922‚Üí**Marker Implementation**
  2923‚Üí
  2924‚Üí```typescript
  2925‚Üíinterface TrainMarker {
  2926‚Üí  trainId: string;
  2927‚Üí  position: [number, number]; // [lng, lat]
  2928‚Üí  status: 'in_service' | 'available' | 'unfit' | 'in_maintenance';
  2929‚Üí  heading?: number; // 0-360 degrees for rotation
  2930‚Üí  lastUpdate: Date;
  2931‚Üí}
  2932‚Üí
  2933‚Üífunction getMarkerColor(status: TrainMarker['status']): string {
  2934‚Üí  const colors = {
  2935‚Üí    in_service: '#2D8A4E',
  2936‚Üí    available: '#2196F3',
  2937‚Üí    unfit: '#E63946',
  2938‚Üí    in_maintenance: '#FF9800',
  2939‚Üí  };
  2940‚Üí  return colors[status];
  2941‚Üí}
  2942‚Üí```

  2971‚Üíinterface DepotMarker {

**Priority Assignment Rationale**

```
P0 Decision Tree:
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Does the client specifically expect this feature?
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Yes ‚Üí P0
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ No ‚Üí Continue

‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Yes ‚Üí P0

‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Yes ‚Üí P0
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ No ‚Üí P1 or P2

P1 Decision Tree:

‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Yes ‚Üí P1
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ No ‚Üí P2
```

**P0 Feature Summary (12 items)**

1. Dashboard with KPI cards
2. Km variance indicator (traffic light)
3. Map with train positions
4. Depot markers on map
5. Allocation recommendation engine
6. Justification display (3+ reasons)
7. One-click allocation
8. Km distribution visualization
9. Train outlier identification
10. Scenario creation
11. Simulation execution
12. Scenario comparison

**P1 Feature Summary (8 items)**

1. Maintenance Gantt chart
2. Drag-drop scheduling
3. Constraint validation
4. Depot capacity heatmap
5. Train list filtering
6. Train details with history
7. Visual alerts system
8. Maintenance type color coding

#### 10.4 Scope Boundaries

**Explicitly IN Scope**

| Area | Included | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Train Allocation | Single-day allocation workflow | One train to one service |
| Km Balancing | Fleet-level visualization | Histogram, rankings, deltas |
| Simulation | What-if scenario system | Create, run, compare |
| Maintenance View | Read and reschedule | Gantt with drag-drop |
| Map | Current positions | Train and depot markers |
| Data | Demo dataset | 80-120 trains, seeded |

**Explicitly OUT of Scope**

| Area | Excluded | Why | Future Phase |
|------|----------|-----|--------------|
| Multi-Day Optimization | MILP solver for week/month | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM Integration | SAP PM, Maximo connections | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption Management | Real-time failure handling | Different architecture | Phase 3 |
| Crew Scheduling | Driver allocation | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native Mobile | iOS/Android apps | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-Language | UI translations | English for demo | Production |
| User Authentication | Login, roles, permissions | Demo runs as single user | Production |
| Audit Trail | Change history logging | Not visible in demo | Production |
| Notification System | Email/SMS alerts | Requires infrastructure | Production |

**Scope Control Principle**

> "When in doubt, leave it out. It's better to demo 6 features that work perfectly than 12 features with bugs."

The scope is intentionally constrained to ensure quality within the 12-week timeline. Any scope addition requires removing something of equal effort.

---

### Chapter 11: Module - Dashboard & KPIs

The Dashboard is the entry point to MMS and must create an immediate positive impression while providing actionable information.

#### 11.1 KPI Cards Specification

#### 11.2 Traffic-Light Indicators

Traffic lights provide instant visual status without requiring interpretation.

**Implementation Specification**

```typescript
type TrafficLightStatus = 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red';

interface TrafficLightConfig {
  metric: string;
  greenThreshold: number;
  yellowThreshold: number;
  direction: 'ascending' | 'descending'; // lower is better vs higher is better
}

const TRAFFIC_LIGHT_CONFIGS: TrafficLightConfig[] = [
  {
    metric: 'kmVariance',
    greenThreshold: 10,
    yellowThreshold: 15,
    direction: 'descending', // lower variance is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'fitPercentage',
    greenThreshold: 90,
    yellowThreshold: 80,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher fit % is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'allocationRate',
    greenThreshold: 95,
    yellowThreshold: 85,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher allocation is better
  },
];
```

**Visual Representation**

```
Green (ūüüĘ):  Background: #238823, Border: #1a6b1a
Yellow (ūüü°): Background: #FFBF00, Border: #cc9900
Red (ūüĒī):    Background: #D2222D, Border: #a61b24
```

**Placement Rules**

- Traffic lights appear in the top-right of each KPI card
- Size: 12px diameter circle
- On hover: Show tooltip explaining status
- On click: Navigate to detail view for that metric

**Capacity Calculation**

```typescript
interface DepotCapacity {
  depotId: string;
  date: Date;
  tracksAvailable: number;
  tracksScheduled: number;
  utilizationPercent: number; // (scheduled / available) * 100
}

function getCapacityColor(utilization: number): string {
  if (utilization < 50) return COLORS.LOW;      // Light gray
  if (utilization < 80) return COLORS.MEDIUM;   // Amber
  return COLORS.HIGH;                            // Red
}
```

**Interaction**

| Hover cell | Tooltip: "Porto - Wednesday: 3/5 tracks scheduled (60%)" |
| Click cell | Navigate to Gantt view filtered to that depot/date |
| Click depot name | Navigate to depot detail page |

interface Alert {

### Chapter 12: Module - Map & Fleet Visualization

The map provides operational awareness and visual proof of fleet tracking capability.

#### 12.1 Map Provider & Configuration

**Recommended Provider: Mapbox GL JS**

| Aspect | Configuration |
|--------|---------------|
| Provider | Mapbox GL JS v3 |
| Style | Dark mode: `mapbox://styles/mapbox/dark-v11` |
| Initial Center | Portugal: `[-8.2245, 40.1356]` |
| Initial Zoom | 7 (country-level view) |
| Min Zoom | 5 |

**Alternative: Leaflet with Dark Tiles**

If Mapbox is unavailable, use Leaflet with CartoDB dark tiles:

```typescript
L.tileLayer(
  'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png',
  { attribution: '¬© CartoDB' }
);
```

**Map Container Requirements**

- Full width of content area
- Minimum height: 500px
- Maximum height: 70vh
- Border radius: 8px
- No scroll inside map (panning only)

#### 12.2 Train Markers & Status Colors

**Marker Design**

```
Train Marker (16x16px SVG):
‚ĒĆ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēź
‚Ēā ‚Ė∂‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ  ‚Ēā  (Simplified train icon)
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēė
```

**Status Color Mapping**

| Status | Color | Hex Code | Meaning |
|--------|-------|----------|---------|
| Fit (In Service) | Green | `#2D8A4E` | Operating normally |
| Fit (Available) | Blue | `#2196F3` | Ready for allocation |
| Unfit | Red | `#E63946` | Requires maintenance |
| In Maintenance | Orange | `#FF9800` | Currently at depot |

**Marker Implementation**

```typescript
interface TrainMarker {
  trainId: string;
  position: [number, number]; // [lng, lat]
  status: 'in_service' | 'available' | 'unfit' | 'in_maintenance';
  heading?: number; // 0-360 degrees for rotation
  lastUpdate: Date;
}

function getMarkerColor(status: TrainMarker['status']): string {
  const colors = {
    in_service: '#2D8A4E',
    available: '#2196F3',
    unfit: '#E63946',
    in_maintenance: '#FF9800',
  };
  return colors[status];
}
```

On hover, show abbreviated tooltip:
```

‚Ēā 39,600 km | ūüüĘ Fit  ‚Ēā
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēė
```

#### 12.3 Depot Markers & Capacity Indicators

**Depot Marker Design**

**Capacity Ring Visualization**

Each depot marker has a ring showing utilization:

```typescript
interface DepotMarker {
  depotId: string;
  name: string;
  position: [number, number];
  capacityTracks: number;
  scheduledTracks: number;
  utilizationPercent: number;
}

function getCapacityRingColor(utilization: number): string {
  if (utilization < 60) return '#2D8A4E';  // Green
  if (utilization < 90) return '#FF9800';  // Orange
  return '#E63946';                         // Red
}
```

  3755‚Üí
  3756‚Üí#### 15.3 Drag-Drop Scheduling
  3757‚Üí
  3758‚Üí**Drag States**
  3759‚Üí
  3760‚Üí```typescript
  3761‚Üítype DragState =
  3762‚Üí  | { type: 'idle' }
  3763‚Üí  | { type: 'dragging'; blockId: string; originalDate: Date }
  3764‚Üí  | { type: 'hovering'; blockId: string; targetDate: Date; isValid: boolean };
  3765‚Üí```
  3766‚Üí
  3767‚Üí**Drag Visual Feedback**
  3768‚Üí
  3769‚Üí```
  3770‚ÜíDuring drag:
  3771‚Üí- Source cell: Dashed outline (ghost)
  3772‚Üí- Dragged block: Semi-transparent (opacity: 0.7)
  3773‚Üí- Valid drop zone: Green highlight
  3774‚Üí- Invalid drop zone: Red highlight + X cursor
  3775‚Üí```
  3776‚Üí
  3777‚Üí**Drop Zone Validation**
  3778‚Üí
  3779‚Üí```typescript
  3780‚Üíinterface DropValidation {
  3781‚Üí  isValid: boolean;
  3782‚Üí  reason?: string;
  3783‚Üí}
  3784‚Üí
  3785‚Üífunction validateDrop(block: MaintenanceBlock, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string): DropValidation {
  3786‚Üí  // Check depot capacity
  3787‚Üí  const capacity = getDepotCapacity(targetDepot, targetDate);
  3788‚Üí  if (capacity.utilized >= capacity.total) {
  3789‚Üí    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Depot at full capacity' };
  3790‚Üí  }
  3791‚Üí
  3792‚Üí  // Check maintenance interval
  3793‚Üí  const lastMaintenance = getLastMaintenance(block.trainId, block.type);
  3794‚Üí  const kmSinceLast = getCurrentKm(block.trainId) - lastMaintenance.km;
  3795‚Üí  if (kmSinceLast < getMinInterval(block.type)) {
  3796‚Üí    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Too early for this maintenance type' };
  3797‚Üí  }
  3798‚Üí
  3799‚Üí  // Check train availability
  3800‚Üí  const allocation = getAllocation(block.trainId, targetDate);
  3801‚Üí  if (allocation) {
  3802‚Üí    return { isValid: false, reason: `Train allocated to ${allocation.serviceCode}` };
  3803‚Üí  }
  3804‚Üí
  3805‚Üí  return { isValid: true };
  3806‚Üí}
  3807‚Üí```
  3808‚Üí
  3809‚Üí**Drag Completion**
  3810‚Üí
  3811‚ÜíOn valid drop:
  3812‚Üí1. Show confirmation toast: "PM1 for UTE-3400-012 moved to Jan 18"
  3813‚Üí2. Update database via PATCH /api/maintenance/{id}
  3814‚Üí3. Refresh Gantt view
  3815‚Üí4. Update depot capacity indicators
  3816‚Üí
  3817‚ÜíOn invalid drop:
  3818‚Üí1. Block snaps back to original position
  3819‚Üí2. Show error toast with reason
  3820‚Üí3. Highlight constraint violation
  3821‚Üí
  3822‚Üí#### 15.4 Constraint Validation
  3823‚Üí
  3824‚Üí**Constraint Types**
  3825‚Üí
  3826‚Üí| Constraint | Validation | Error Message |
  3827‚Üí|------------|------------|---------------|
  3828‚Üí| Depot Capacity | Max simultaneous maintenances | "Depot {name} at capacity on {date}" |
  3829‚Üí| Train Availability | No service allocation conflict | "Train allocated to {service} on {date}" |
  3830‚Üí| Maintenance Interval | Minimum km between same type | "{type} interval not yet reached" |
  3831‚Üí| Duration Fit | Block fits within selected period | "Maintenance spans beyond view period" |
  3832‚Üí
  3833‚Üí**Real-Time Validation**
  3834‚Üí
  3835‚Üí```typescript
  3836‚Üí// Validate as user drags
  3837‚Üífunction onDragOver(blockId: string, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string) {
  3838‚Üí  const validation = validateDrop(
  3839‚Üí    getBlock(blockId),
  3840‚Üí    targetDate,
  3841‚Üí    targetDepot
  3842‚Üí  );
  3843‚Üí
  3844‚Üí  setDropIndicator({
  3845‚Üí    date: targetDate,
  3846‚Üí    depot: targetDepot,
  3847‚Üí    isValid: validation.isValid,
  3848‚Üí    message: validation.reason,
  3849‚Üí  });
  3850‚Üí}
  3851‚Üí```

file.content:
```
1. User clicks "New Scenario"
2. System copies current state as baseline
3. User makes adjustments (allocation changes)
4. User clicks "Run Simulation"
5. System projects 30-day impact
6. User views results
7. User decides: Apply or Discard
```

**Scenario Data Model**

```typescript
interface Scenario {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string;
  baselineDate: Date;
  status: 'draft' | 'simulated' | 'applied' | 'discarded';
  adjustments: ScenarioAdjustment[];
  results?: SimulationResults;
  createdAt: Date;
  createdBy: string;
}

interface ScenarioAdjustment {
  type: 'allocation_change' | 'maintenance_reschedule';
  trainId: string;
  serviceId?: string;
  newDate?: Date;
  description: string;
}
```

#### 14.5 Simulation Execution

**Simulation Process**

```
POST /api/scenarios/{id}/simulate

1. Load scenario adjustments
2. Project train km for next 30 days
3. Calculate impact metrics
4. Compare with baseline
5. Return results
```

**Simulation Results**

```typescript
interface SimulationResults {
  projectionDays: number;
  baseline: FleetMetrics;
  projected: FleetMetrics;
  impact: {
    varianceChange: number;      // -4.3% (variance reduced)
    deadRunningChange: number;   // +3.2% (dead running increased)
    overRunnerCount: number;
    underRunnerCount: number;
  };
  trainProjections: TrainProjection[];
}

interface FleetMetrics {
  averageKm: number;
  variance: number;
  standardDeviation: number;
  totalDeadRunning: number;
}

interface TrainProjection {
  trainId: string;
  currentKm: number;
  projectedKm: number;
  currentDelta: number;
  projectedDelta: number;
}
```

During simulation (typically 5-10 seconds):

interface MaintenanceBlock {

#### 15.3 Drag-Drop Scheduling

**Drag States**

```typescript
type DragState =
  | { type: 'idle' }
  | { type: 'dragging'; blockId: string; originalDate: Date }
  | { type: 'hovering'; blockId: string; targetDate: Date; isValid: boolean };
```

**Drag Visual Feedback**

```
During drag:
- Source cell: Dashed outline (ghost)
- Dragged block: Semi-transparent (opacity: 0.7)
- Valid drop zone: Green highlight
- Invalid drop zone: Red highlight + X cursor
```

**Drop Zone Validation**

```typescript
interface DropValidation {
  isValid: boolean;
  reason?: string;
}

function validateDrop(block: MaintenanceBlock, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string): DropValidation {
  // Check depot capacity
  const capacity = getDepotCapacity(targetDepot, targetDate);
  if (capacity.utilized >= capacity.total) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Depot at full capacity' };
  }

  // Check maintenance interval
  const lastMaintenance = getLastMaintenance(block.trainId, block.type);
  const kmSinceLast = getCurrentKm(block.trainId) - lastMaintenance.km;
  if (kmSinceLast < getMinInterval(block.type)) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Too early for this maintenance type' };
  }

  // Check train availability
  const allocation = getAllocation(block.trainId, targetDate);
  if (allocation) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: `Train allocated to ${allocation.serviceCode}` };
  }

  return { isValid: true };
}
```

**Drag Completion**

On valid drop:
1. Show confirmation toast: "PM1 for UTE-3400-012 moved to Jan 18"
2. Update database via PATCH /api/maintenance/{id}
3. Refresh Gantt view
4. Update depot capacity indicators

On invalid drop:
1. Block snaps back to original position
2. Show error toast with reason
3. Highlight constraint violation

#### 15.4 Constraint Validation

**Constraint Types**

| Constraint | Validation | Error Message |
|------------|------------|---------------|
| Depot Capacity | Max simultaneous maintenances | "Depot {name} at capacity on {date}" |
| Train Availability | No service allocation conflict | "Train allocated to {service} on {date}" |
| Maintenance Interval | Minimum km between same type | "{type} interval not yet reached" |
| Duration Fit | Block fits within selected period | "Maintenance spans beyond view period" |

**Real-Time Validation**

```typescript
// Validate as user drags
function onDragOver(blockId: string, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string) {
  const validation = validateDrop(
    getBlock(blockId),
    targetDate,
    targetDepot
  );

  setDropIndicator({
    date: targetDate,
    depot: targetDepot,
    isValid: validation.isValid,
    message: validation.reason,
  });
}
```

**Heatmap Cell Data**

```typescript
interface CapacityCell {
  depotId: string;
  date: Date;
  scheduled: number;    // Tracks with maintenance scheduled
  capacity: number;     // Total available tracks
  utilization: number;  // scheduled / capacity * 100
  color: string;        // Based on utilization %
}
```

**Heatmap Interactions**

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Hover cell | Tooltip: "Porto - Jan 15: 5/6 tracks (83%)" |
| Click cell | Scroll Gantt to show that depot's trains |
| Click depot name | Filter Gantt to only that depot |

**Capacity Alert**

When cell is >90% capacity:
- Cell background: Red
- Visual pulse effect
- Tooltip includes: "‚ö†ÔłŹ Consider rescheduling some maintenances"

---

| Blueprint.js | 5.x | Components | Enterprise UI, dark mode |
| @belt/tools | 0.0.1 | State | Slice-based with Immer, avoids React hook pitfalls |
| Recharts | 2.x | Charts | Simple API, React integration |
| Mapbox GL JS | 3.x | Maps | Performance, dark mode style |

**Backend Stack**

| Technology | Version | Purpose | Decision Rationale |
|------------|---------|---------|-------------------|
